visualization.title: Average Reported Workload Hours per Course

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       AVG(ce.workload_hours) AS avg_workload_hours
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses AS c ON cs.course_id = c.id
WHERE ce.workload_hours IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.code, c.name
ORDER BY c.code ASC;

issues: SQL GROUP BY includes both course_code and course_name; if course_code is guaranteed unique this is redundant (harmless) but could be simplified to group by course_code only.
The Vega-Lite spec does not explicitly set sort on the x axis; it likely preserves the SQL ORDER BY, but adding an explicit sort in the x encoding would ensure consistent ordering.
avg_workload_hours values are high-precision in the sample (many decimals); consider applying numeric formatting/rounding for improved readability on the y axis or tooltip.

visualization.title: Average Reported Workload Hours per Course

result.sql:
SELECT c.code AS course_code,
       c.name AS course_name,
       AVG(ce.workload_hours) AS avg_workload_hours
FROM public.course_evaluations AS ce
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.courses AS c ON cs.course_id = c.id
WHERE ce.workload_hours IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.code, c.name
ORDER BY c.code ASC;

issues: SQL GROUP BY includes both course_code and course_name; if course_code is guaranteed unique this is redundant (harmless) but could be simplified to group by course_code only.
The Vega-Lite spec does not explicitly set sort on the x axis; it likely preserves the SQL ORDER BY, but adding an explicit sort in the x encoding would ensure consistent ordering.
avg_workload_hours values are high-precision in the sample (many decimals); consider applying numeric formatting/rounding for improved readability on the y axis or tooltip.
